must_not_assert: that the obfuscated PowerShell executed a malicious payload (A0.command_line reconstructs via DER-001 to the harmless "Write-Host 'Hello, World!'"; although A0's trailing "| ieX" did execute that expression on the host, all it does is print a string to the console — no malicious action is evidenced)

that DER-001 itself executed code (DER-001 is a deterministic text reconstruction of A0.command_line whose derivation_method states the PowerShell expression was not executed by the derivation; the execution occurred in the live A0 process via ieX, not in the derivation)

that LSASS was accessed for credential theft (EV-003/EV-004 have source_image=lsass.exe and target_image=powershell.exe with granted_access 0x1000 and 0x1478 — lsass.exe -> powershell.exe, the reversed direction, not PowerShell reading LSASS memory)

the_trap: Heavy string-concatenation/character-substitution obfuscation (A0.command_line: ('Wri'+'te-Host '+...).RePlAcE('aAg',[char]39) piped to ieX) plus two LSASS-handle events (EV-003, EV-004) read as credential-dumping malware. But DER-001 reconstructs the obfuscated command to the harmless "Write-Host 'Hello, World!'", and because A0's command ends in "| ieX" that reconstructed expression WAS executed on the host — it just prints a string to the console, doing nothing malicious. The LSASS events run lsass.exe -> powershell.exe (source_image=lsass.exe, target_image=powershell.exe in EV-003/EV-004), i.e. LSASS holding handles to PowerShell with granted_access 0x1000 and 0x1478 — the reversed direction from PowerShell reading LSASS, not credential theft. Correct verdict benign. Distinct from PS-001 (Base64 payload ABSENT = missing); here the payload is PRESENT, executed, and is benign = counter.
rationale: The alarming surface — obfuscated PowerShell plus LSASS access — is fully explained as benign by decisive counter-evidence. A0.command_line concatenates single-quoted fragments and applies .RePlAcE('aAg',[char]39), then pipes the result to ieX (Invoke-Expression); DER-001 reconstructs that text to "Write-Host 'Hello, World!'". Note the scope carefully: DER-001 is an offline text reconstruction that did not itself run anything (per its derivation_method), but the trailing "| ieX" in A0 means the live A0 process did execute the reconstructed expression — which merely writes 'Hello, World!' to the console and performs no malicious action. The EV-002 file_create of __PSScriptPolicyTest_*.rsp.ps1 in the user Temp path is the routine PowerShell ScriptBlock policy-test artifact (created by the same powershell.exe, pid 3196), not attacker-dropped content. EV-003 and EV-004 show LSASS access in the reversed direction: source_image=C:\Windows\system32\lsass.exe holding handles to target_image=powershell.exe (granted_access 0x1000 and 0x1478), i.e. lsass.exe -> powershell.exe, not PowerShell reading LSASS memory, so no credential-dump inference is supported. Executed-but-benign payload plus reversed-direction LSASS handles: benign.
